CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2021 Continued To Mar-16-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DON A. ERNST, Esq. of Ernst Law Group (Tel: 805-541-0300) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOSHUA D. KIENITZ, Esq. of Littler Mendelson P.C. (Tel: 415-433-1940) for defendants PG&E Corporation and Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PG&E CORPORATION and PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrers to the complaint's numbered causes of action are ruled on as follows:

1, 3 (hostile work environment harassment and failure to prevent discrimination and harassment): Plaintiff adequately pled that complained of conduct was related to her sex and that it altered terms and conditions of her employment. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 5:14-18, 8:1-3, 10:20-21, 13:8-9.) Demurrer overruled.

5 (retaliation for engaging in a protected activity): Plaintiff adequately pled, inter alia, that she was retaliated against for raising safety concerns at the nuclear power plant where she worked. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 9:11-10:2, 19:20-23.) Labor Code 1102.5 covers an employee's disclosure of "noncompliance with a local, state or federal rule or regulation" without providing that a particular rule or regulation must be identified. Demurrer overruled.

6 (negligent hiring, supervision and/or retention): Plaintiff adequately pled that PG&E "knew or should have known that" its supervisors "were and/or became unfit to perform the work for which they were hired." (Cmplt. 21:14-16.) "[E]ach evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiffs' proof need not be alleged." (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) Harassment and retaliation based on sex discrimination are not normal incidents of employment, so worker's compensation does not apply. Demurrer overruled.

7 (intentional infliction of emotional distress): The complaint does not plead the extreme or outrageous conduct required for this tort. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

8 (negligent infliction of emotional distress): The complaint does not plead the elements of negligence. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
